title: Delicate Herbal Mexican Fish Tamales
description: A light and kid-friendly Mexican dish featuring herbal flavors and buckwheat flour, perfect for a weeknight dinner. This pescatarian-friendly recipe is made with ferment-jang and served in a lazy-susan-spinner vessel.
instructions: Soak the corn husks in water for 30 minutes to make them pliable.
In a large bowl, whisk together the buckwheat flour, ferment-jang, and a pinch of salt.
Add the chopped cilantro, lime juice, olive oil, and mix until a dough forms.
Divide the dough into 12 equal pieces and roll each into a ball.
Flatten each ball into a thin circle and place a piece of cod fillet in the center.
Fold the sides of the dough over the filling and roll up the tamale, folding the top over to seal.
Place the tamales in a large steamer basket and steam for 15 minutes.
Serve the tamales hot with a sprinkle of dried oregano and a squeeze of lime juice.
